# Doubt Bureau Casebook

> A bilingual retro-mystery game for critical-thinking practice. It contains
> four fixed browser excerpts—Drill, Scene, an Expedition Forecaster excerpt,
> and a two-step Detective dependency demo—plus an optional server-backed Daily
> case. The fixed routes do not call a model.

- [Product, i18n, and local-data notes](/README.md)
- [Art direction](/assets/art/ART_DIRECTION.md)
- [Art manifest and generation prompts](/assets/art/manifest.json)
- [Daily deployment and data boundary](/docs/DAILY_DEPLOYMENT.md)

Player progress is stored only in browser localStorage. Never provide this site
with a chat password, cookie, or session token.

The Web game is a third entry point; it does not replace the repository’s
Claude Code/CLI or Skill ZIP routes. The locale registry is
`/content/demo-cases.js`, learner-facing UI strings are in
`/content/ui-copy.js`, and fixed English/Taiwan Traditional Chinese content is
under `/content/locales/`. Daily public prompts are under `/content/daily/`.
Unpublished Daily cases, answer records, and credentials belong only in private
storage and never in a public API payload.

The checked-in seven-day Daily fixtures mirror public offline examples, so they
demonstrate response shaping and payload leak prevention—not anti-cheat
secrecy. Production scored cases need independent keys that never ship to the
browser or public repository.
